name: Emergency Operations Post — West Bank Radio Relay
description: A National Guard communications tent on the west bank of the river, accessible only through radio contact — the receiving end of Ida Broussard's transmission, staffed by net control operator Sergeant Dwayne Mouton.
atmosphericDetails: The frequency opens at fifteen-minute intervals — the next window is in eleven minutes and then again in twenty-six
Sergeant Mouton's voice on the frequency is calm and methodical — he has been doing this for five days and has learned to hear past the QRN
The post's own battery backup is at 60% — enough for another four hours of operation, which feels like luxury from the Algiers side
